intro: Andryala pontana, also known as Pontic hedge trefoil, is a perennial plant species in the sunflower family (Asteraceae) that is native to the Mediterranean region and parts of Eastern Europe. Traditionally, Andryala pontana has been used to treat various health issues, including fever, respiratory problems, and gastrointestinal disorders, due to its anti-inflammatory, antiseptic, and antimicrobial properties. The bioactive constituents of this plant include flavonoids, phenolic acids, and terpenoids, which are responsible for its medicinal properties. Andryala pontana is often used to make herbal teas, infusions, and tinctures, which are commonly used to treat respiratory issues and to promote overall well-being.
